title: GoDaddy Inc. (GDDY) Intrinsic Value
description: An independent discounted cash flow (DCF) intrinsic value analysis of GoDaddy Inc. (GDDY) compiled entirely by Gemini.

fcfGrowth.title: FCF Growth Rate (Y1-Y5)
fcfGrowth.rationale: A 6.8% growth rate mirrors the company's recent topline performance. This assumption is supported by the continued, steady growth in its massive domain and hosting install base, paired with the higher-margin expansion of its 'Applications & Commerce' software segment.

discountRate.title: Discount Rate (WACC)
discountRate.rationale: An 8.8% discount rate is utilized, reflecting a beta slightly below the market average (0.979). It accounts for the relatively stable recurring revenue of domain renewals offset by intense competition in the broader SaaS and website building spaces.

terminalGrowth.title: Terminal Growth Rate
terminalGrowth.rationale: A 2.5% terminal growth rate assumes GoDaddy will grow slightly above core inflation and historical GDP, reflecting the enduring need for digital identities (domains) and basic web infrastructure for businesses indefinitely.

thesisTitle: The Cash Flow Engine for Micro-Businesses
thesisParagraphs: GoDaddy is fundamentally misunderstood if viewed simply as a cheap place to buy a URL. The core value lies in its position at the absolute top of the funnel for micro-business formation. By capturing a customer at the moment of inception—registering a domain name—GoDaddy creates an immediate, highly sticky relationship. The true economic engine engages when the company successfully cross-sells higher margin services like email, security, and website building tools into that massive install base.

This model is incredibly capital-light and generates an impressive $1.3 billion in annual free cash flow. Management has demonstrated discipline, aggressively utilizing this cash flow to consistently repurchase shares rather than pursuing dilutive, speculative acquisitions. Given its strong recurring revenue, a 6.8% steady growth rate, and a reasonable cost of capital, GoDaddy appears substantially undervalued by the market, trading more like a legacy telecom than a highly profitable, scalable software platform.
verdictFooter: Based on latest available data. Data via S&P Global (Mar 20, 2026).

faq.question: Why did Gemini pick a 6.8% growth rate for GoDaddy?
faq.answer: The model directly utilizes the recent 6.8% revenue growth figure, projecting that GoDaddy's strategy of monetizing its existing domain customer base with higher-margin software services (Applications & Commerce) will continue to drive steady, mid-single-digit free cash flow expansion.

faq.question: What discount rate was used for GoDaddy's DCF?
faq.answer: An 8.8% discount rate was selected. This is driven by its beta (0.979), indicating its volatility is roughly in line with the broader market, balancing its highly recurring domain renewal revenue against execution risks in its competitive software segments.

faq.question: Does this model factor in Shopify or Wix?
faq.answer: Implicitly, yes. While GoDaddy faces intense competition from these specialized platforms, the 6.8% growth rate reflects its success despite them. The model assumes GoDaddy maintains its strength primarily among micro-businesses and 'do-it-for-me' customers, rather than attempting to dominate complex, high-end e-commerce.
